seoTitle: How to Cancel Your Coursera Subscription Step by Step
metaDescription: Cancel a Coursera subscription in a few clicks by heading to account settings, finding your plan, and confirming the change before your next billing date.
intro: Whether your priorities have shifted or you just want to pause your learning for a while, canceling a Coursera subscription is a quick process once you know where to look. This walks through accessing your account settings, finding the subscription options, and confirming the cancellation.

steps.heading: Go to your Coursera account settings
steps.body: Sign in and navigate to the settings area where your subscription details are managed.

steps.heading: Find your subscription options
steps.body: Locate the section listing your current plan and the option to cancel it.

steps.heading: Confirm your cancellation
steps.body: Follow the prompts to confirm you want to end the subscription, reviewing any details Coursera shows about your remaining access.

steps.heading: Note what happens to your course progress
steps.body: Check how cancellation affects any courses you're partway through so you can plan whether to finish them first.

faq.question: Do I lose my course progress if I cancel?
faq.answer: Your progress record is generally saved, but active access to subscription-only content typically ends once the current billing period is over.

faq.question: How long before renewal should I cancel?
faq.answer: It's safest to cancel a few days before your next billing date to make sure the change processes in time.

faq.question: Are there alternatives to canceling completely?
faq.answer: Depending on your plan, you may be able to switch to a different option instead of canceling outright, so it's worth checking what's available.

faq.question: Can I resubscribe after canceling?
faq.answer: Yes, you can typically sign back up for a Coursera subscription whenever you're ready to continue learning.
